steps: Log in to your WordPress admin dashboard at https://{your-store-domain}/wp-admin
Navigate to WooCommerce > Settings > Advanced > REST API
Enter a description (e.g. 'Agent Gateway'), select the WordPress user, and set Permissions to 'Read/Write'
Immediately copy the Consumer Key and Consumer Secret — the secret will not be shown again
Use the Consumer Key as the API key in requests (via HTTP Basic Auth or as query parameters)

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: Consumer Key
credential_fields.description: Your WooCommerce REST API Consumer Key (used as the username in HTTP Basic Auth)

notes: Replace {store_domain} with your actual WordPress/WooCommerce store domain
WooCommerce is self-hosted — each store has its own API keys; there is no central developer portal
The Consumer Secret is only shown once at key generation — store it securely
For HTTPS stores, use HTTP Basic Auth with Consumer Key as username and Consumer Secret as password
The base URL is specific to your store: https://{store-domain}/wp-json/wc/v3
WooCommerce must be installed and active on a WordPress site

test_endpoint.description: Retrieve system status to verify the API credentials
